sutta: monks these 5 are not a good man's gifts but 5 he gives without deference gives without thinking gives not with his own hand gives 1 is rejected or thrown away he gives holding views as to the future verily monks these are the 5 that's the end of the sutta
commentary: this describes how in giving are these these are the wrong ways to give like the first 1 is to give without deference that means without respect some people they they give like to a beggar they they give 10 cents or 20 cents they throw it at the burger that's not good because if you give without respect then there is there is there is uh the uh fruit of it a result of that but that's in the next i want to explain here the second 1 is give without thinking that means mindlessly without paying attention mine is somewhere else when you give the third 1 does not give it his own hand that means it is better if you give yourself but it's not always practical sometimes you're not able to go and give yourself then you ask somebody to give it on your behalf it's also quite all right because if if you can't go yourself not to give then the fourth 1 he gives what is rejected or thrown away that's not good at least if what is extra still in good condition that's all right but uh some people uh like i've gone on arms around once and when i was living in ipoh and okay i went to this new village for arms round and those people had never seen a mango and arms around so some of them started to give 1 day i got a piece of bread and with mold on it i literally found this on it so uh it's not good to give what you want to throw away and then the last 1 to give holding views as to the future that means some people give it expectations they think some people editing when i give i should make a wish but it's not very good because there's a selfish thought you're giving like trading giving in hopes of getting something in return and that's not that's not so good because there is a little bit of selfishness in it so the blessing the merit is not so great the next futa is
